provenance.source_corpus: Comparative performance of cover systems to prevent acid mine drainage from pre-oxidized tailings: A numerical hydro-geochemical assessment

nodes.name: well-designed three-layer cover with capillary barrier effects (CCBE)

source_spans.text: The simulations show, however, that a well-designed three-layer cover with capillary barrier effects (CCBE) would be efficient in reducing the oxygen flux and AMD generation, even in the case of highly pre-oxidized tailings.

The simulations show, however, that a well-designed three-layer cover with capillary barrier effects (CCBE) would be efficient in reducing the oxygen flux and AMD generation, even in the case of highly pre-oxidized tailings.

The simulations show, however, that a well-designed three-layer cover with capillary barrier effects (CCBE) would be efficient in reducing the oxygen flux and AMD generation, even in the case of highly pre-oxidized tailings.
The deeper the water table, the more AMD is generated.
Evaporation can significantly decrease the degree of saturation close to the surface.
Evaporation can significantly decrease the degree of saturation close to the surface.
Results indicate that a thicker cover is generally more efficient at limiting oxygen ingress.
Results indicate that a thicker cover is generally more efficient at limiting oxygen ingress.

The reclamation of old mine sites with pre-oxidized tailings is particularly challenging because of indirect oxidation reactions which can limit the overall effectiveness of an oxygen barrier to prevent AMD.

The reclamation of old mine sites with pre-oxidized tailings is particularly challenging because of indirect oxidation reactions which can limit the overall effectiveness of an oxygen barrier to prevent AMD.

The simulations indicate that because of reduced cover effectiveness with pre-oxidized tailings, the general design targets developed for non-oxidized tailings may not always be directly applicable to already oxidized tailings.

nodes.name: general design targets developed for non-oxidized tailings

source_spans.text: The simulations indicate that because of reduced cover effectiveness with pre-oxidized tailings, the general design targets developed for non-oxidized tailings may not always be directly applicable to already oxidized tailings.

The results indicate that under a given set of conditions, a bilayer cover (with a capillary break above the reactive tailings) would not significantly improve cover performance (compared to a monolayer cover) due to water losses by evaporation.

The results indicate that under a given set of conditions, a bilayer cover (with a capillary break above the reactive tailings) would not significantly improve cover performance (compared to a monolayer cover) due to water losses by evaporation.

A two-month drought would significantly decrease the degree of saturation in the monolayer cover CA and the related annual oxygen flux to 179, 100 and 49 g/m2/y for a 1, 2 and 4 m thick cover, respectively
